channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Suspect arrested in arson fire that forced Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro, family to flee residence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and broke into the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion where he set a fire that left significant damage and forced Democratic Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 6 injured after explosion, house collapse in Austin
item.description: One house was "leveled" while another "suffered severe collapse damage" following an explosion in Austin on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, Fox 5

item.title: US still won't say whether it will return mistakenly deported man, despite Supreme Court decision
item.description: The Trump administration is doubling down on its decision not to tell a federal court whether it has any plans to repatriate a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported last month and remains confined in a notorious prison in El Salvador, despite a Supreme Court ruling and lower court order that the
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing Shreveport 12-year-old: Louisiana police ask for public's help
item.description: 12-year-old Zyra'Kem Copeland was last seen on Saturday, April 12. He was riding a red bicycle when he left.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Woods, NewsNation

item.title: 3 cases of rare brain disease reported in Oregon
item.description: Three cases of a rare brain disease have been reported by public health officials in Hood River County.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Flanigan, ABC 4

item.title: Wisconsin teen charged in parents’ deaths is accused of plotting to kill Trump
item.description: A Wisconsin teenager charged in the deaths of his parents faces wider allegations that he killed them to “obtain the financial means” to assassinate President Donald Trump and overthrow the government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The hierarchy behind the Hawaiian shirts at Trader Joe's
item.description: If you’re shopping at Trader Joe’s and you need the kind of assistance that only a seasoned, veteran TJ’s employee can provide, you might want to look at their shirts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump tariffs bring Democrat disagreements to surface
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariffs are putting Democrats in a challenging political position as they decide how strongly to denounce his trade policy.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The hierarchy behind the Hawaiian shirts at Trader Joe's
item.description: If you’re shopping at Trader Joe’s and you need the kind of assistance that only a seasoned, veteran TJ’s employee can provide, you might want to look at their shirts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: In a report released Sunday, the doctor said in a summary that Trump is “fully fit to execute the duties of Commander-in-Chief and Head of State.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fire at Pennsylvania Governor's Residence was 'act of arson': State Police
item.description: Police said Governor Josh Shapiro and his family were able to safely evacuate after a fire — believed to be an "act of arson" — broke out at the Harrisburg mansion.
item.dc:creator: Hayden Sherry, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on how flat funding set stage for Texas measles outbreak, might fuel more
item.description: The measles outbreak in West Texas didn’t happen just by chance. The easily preventable disease, declared eliminated in the U.S. in 2000, ripped through Texas communities in part because health departments were starved of the funding needed to run vaccine programs, officials say.
item.dc:creator: LAURA UNGAR, MICHELLE R. SMITH and DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Understanding mutual funds: A beginner's guide to investing
item.description: Despite recent stock market slumps and fears in the investment world, it's never a bad time to learn about strategies to boost your investing experience.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump sits cageside at Miami UFC event in his latest appearance at a sports event
item.description: Trump shook hands with some supporters as he walked to his cageside seat Saturday night at Miami’s Kaseya Center while others waved his trademark red campaign cap.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Masters patrons rediscover the joy of old-school communication
item.description: The Masters golf tournament is unique in that it does not allow cell phones, providing a chance to make free calls to anywhere in the world, and allows for a break from the outside world
item.dc:creator: Brad Means, Fox 5

item.title: Pet food recalled after testing positive for salmonella and listeria: FDA
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration has announced a voluntary recall of certain puppy and kitten foods.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KTLA 5

item.title: Intellectually disabled teen shot by Idaho police dies after being removed from life support
item.description: An autistic, nonverbal teenage boy who was shot repeatedly by Idaho police from the other side of a chain-link fence while he was holding a knife died Saturday after being removed from life support, his family said.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kansas man who was falsely accused of being a shooter after Chiefs’ 2024 Super Bowl win has died
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — A Kansas man who was falsely accused of being among the shooters who opened fire at a rally celebrating the Kansas City Chiefs’ Super Bowl victory last year has died, his attorney said. Denton Loudermill Jr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Popular soup, bowl products may be contaminated; USDA issues public health alert
item.description: The USDA has issued a nationwide public health alert warning consumers that some popular soup and bowl products could be contaminated with wood.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Trump exempts phones, computers, other electronics from reciprocal tariffs
item.description: The Trump administration is exempting electronics such as phones, computers and other electronics from the "reciprocal" tariffs imposed on other nations, including China.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Man wrongly accused in Chiefs parade shooting found dead
item.description: An Olathe, Kansas, man who was wrongly accused of being a "shooter" and "illegal alien" at the Chiefs Super Bowl rally in 2024 has died.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, NewsNation

item.title: An emboldened anti-abortion faction wants women who have abortions to face criminal charges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As Kristan Hawkins, president of the national anti-abortion group Students for Life, tours college campuses, she has grown accustomed to counterprotests from abortion rights activists.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US may already be in recession: BlackRock CEO
item.description: BlackRock CEO Larry Fink said during a recent interview that the United States may already be in a recession or getting very close to one as a result of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Laredo OKs giving state city land to build border wall
item.description: The City of Laredo is allowing the State of Texas a small section of city-owned land near an international bridge to build a segment of border wall.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Trump eyes major cuts to NOAA research
item.description: The Trump administration is eyeing cuts to climate, weather and ocean research in a draft budget blueprint for the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Schools lined up for help getting cleaner school buses. Then came the EPA freeze
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Looking to cut pollution from its fleet of diesel-burning school buses, an Oklahoma school district last year doubled its electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tufts student from Turkey details arrest, crowded detention conditions in new court filing
item.description: A Tufts University doctoral student from Turkey is demanding her release after she was detained by immigration officials near her Massachusetts home, detailing how she was scared when the men grabbed her phone and feared she would be killed.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department tasked with enforcing Trump's transgender sports ban in Maine
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — Maine officials said Friday the state will not comply with a ban on transgender athletes in high school sports in the wake of a Trump administration finding that the state violated antidiscrimination laws by allowing the students to participate. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Immigration judge allows Mahmoud Khalil deportation effort to proceed
item.description: A judge in former Columbia University activist Mahmoud Khalil's case ruled against him Friday, finding he is eligible for deportation while giving his legal team a little less than two weeks to respond.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: Black Power Ranger actor disagrees with writer on his casting
item.description: Walter Emanual Jones, the actor who portrayed the Black Power Ranger in the "Mighty Morphin Power Rangers," responded to a statement made by one of the series' writers calling the casting of a Black actor in the role a "mistake.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Doctors remove pig kidney from an Alabama woman after a record 130 days
item.description: An Alabama woman who lived with a pig kidney for a record 130 days had the organ removed after her body began rejecting it and is back on dialysis, doctors announced Friday – a disappointment in the ongoing quest for animal-to-human transplants.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amateur golfer urinates in creek at Masters Tournament, gets round of applause
item.description: U.S. Amateur champion Jose Luis Ballester had to take a different kind of relief Thursday at the Masters, just not the kind found in the Rules of Golf.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NYC sightseeing helicopter plunges into river, killing 6 including Spanish family and pilot
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New York City sightseeing helicopter broke apart in midair Thursday and crashed upside-down into the Hudson River, killing the pilot and a family of five Spanish tourists in the latest U.S. aviation disaster, officials said.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ and TED SHAFFREY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House passes budget framework
item.description: President Donald Trump's agenda is now one step closer to getting funding from Congress because the House passed a budget framework.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Brandt, Fox 5

item.title: Johnson presses ahead with vote on Trump budget blueprint
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said he was pressing forward with a vote Thursday morning on the party's “one big, beautiful bill” full of President Trump’s legislative agenda, holding a joint appearance with Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9
